post.summary: Unlink offences from schedules - will also unlink any associated inchoate offences (i.e. if any of the passed in offences have children they will also be unlinked)
Link offence to a schedule part - will also link any associated inchoate offences (i.e. if  passed in offence has children they will also be linked)

post.description: Reactivate offences in NOMIS, only to be used for offences that are end dated but NOMIS need them to be reactivated

post.summary: Create encouragement offence for parent offence
post.description: Encouragement offence creates a new record with existing parent offence value, but with 'E' suffix to the offence code
Deactivate offences in NOMIS, only to be used for offences that are end dated but are active in NOMIS

get.summary: Retrieve all schedule 19ZA offence codes by part number
get.description: Returns list of offence codes broken down by schedule part, commonly used for TORERA offences

get.summary: Determine both if the passed in offence codes are related to the PCSC list and whether they have any exclusions to be considered for SDS40 and Progression Model
get.description: This endpoint will return a list of offences with PCSC markers and any SDS early release exclusions if applicable.

get.summary: Determine if the passed in offence codes are to be excluded from early release due to being Sexual, Violent, Domestic Abuse, National Security or Terrorism.
get.description: This endpoint will return a list of offences and the exclusion category if applicable.

get.summary: Retrieves the lists of all the offences that are to be excluded from early release.
get.description: This returns five lists for Sexual, Violent, Domestic Abuse, National Security or Terrorism offences.
This endpoint will return all four PCSC lists

get.summary: Determine if the passed in offence codes are related to any of the PCSC lists
get.description: This endpoint will return a list of offences and whether they are im any of the PCSC lists

get.summary: Get offence matching the passed ID - with schedule related data
get.description: This endpoint will return the offence that matches the unique ID passed in

get.summary: Get schedule by ID - includes all scheduled parts and mapped offences
Find all schedules - does not include mapped offences
Get all offences matching the passed offence code, does a start with match
get.description: This endpoint will return the offences that start with the passed offence code

get.summary: Get the results of the most recent load
get.description: Get the results of the most recent load

get.summary: Get offence matching the passed ID
get.description: This endpoint will return the offence that matches the unique ID passed in

get.summary: Get the HO Code associated with an offence code
get.description: This endpoint will return the HO Code associated with an offence code, could return null

200.description: Offence code exists and associated hoCode returned (could be null/empty)

404.description: No offence exists for the passed in offence code

get.summary: Get all offences matching the passed offence code, does a start with match
get.description: This endpoint will return the offences that start with the passed offence code

get.summary: Get the unique offence matching the passed offence code
get.description: This endpoint will return the offence that matches the unique code passed in

get.summary: Get a list of offences that match the passed in list of offence codes
get.description: This endpoint will return the offences that match the codes passed in

get.summary: Get offence code to actuarial weighting mapping
get.description: Queries database to get offence code to actuarial weighting mapping

get.summary: Fetch changes pushed to NOMIS between a from and to date range (to defaults to now)
Get values of all feature toggles

feature.description: Feature to be toggled: FULL_SYNC_NOMIS, DELTA_SYNC_NOMIS, FULL_SYNC_SDRS or DELTA_SYNC_SDRS

enabled.description: true or false - depending on whether the feature should be enabled

SchedulePartIdAndOffenceId.description: Schedule part ID and Offence ID - used for unlinking offences from schedules

lineReference.description: The line reference for the associated schedule's legislation

legislationText.description: The legislation text for the associated schedule

paragraphTitle.description: Schedule paragraph title that this offence is mapped to

paragraphNumber.description: Schedule paragraph number that this offence is mapped to

description.description: The offence description (taken from SDRS CJSTitle field)

LinkedScheduleDetails.description: Schedule details when associated to an offence

description.description: The offence description (taken from SDRS CJSTitle field)

revisionId.description: The revision number of the offence

homeOfficeStatsCode.description: The offence's home office stats code

changedDate.description: The date this offence was last changed in SDRS

loadDate.description: The date this offence was loaded into manage-offences from SDRS

schedules.description: The schedules linked to this offence

isChild.description: If true then this is a inchoate offence; i.e. a child of another offence

parentOffenceId.description: The parent offence id of an inchoate offence

childOffences.description: A list of child offence ID's; i.e. inchoate offences linked to this offence

legislation.description: The legislation associated to this offence (from actsAndSections in the SDRS response)

maxPeriodIsLife.description: Set to true if max period is life

maxPeriodOfIndictmentYears.description: Set to the max period of indictment in years

maxPeriodOfIndictmentMonths.description: Set to the max period of indictment in months

maxPeriodOfIndictmentWeeks.description: Set to the max period of indictment in weeks

maxPeriodOfIndictmentDays.description: Set to the max period of indictment in days

lineReference.description: The line reference for the associated schedule's legislation

legislationText.description: The legislation text for the associated schedule

paragraphTitle.description: Schedule paragraph title that this offence is mapped to

paragraphNumber.description: Schedule paragraph number that this offence is mapped to

SchedulePart.description: Schedule part details and associated offences

description.description: The offence description (taken from SDRS CJSTitle field)

revisionId.description: The revision number of the offence

homeOfficeStatsCode.description: The offence's home office stats code

changedDate.description: The date this offence was last changed in SDRS

loadDate.description: The date this offence was loaded into manage-offences from SDRS

schedules.description: The schedules linked to this offence

isChild.description: If true then this is a inchoate offence; i.e. a child of another offence

parentOffenceId.description: The parent offence id of an inchoate offence

childOffenceIds.description: A list of child offence ID's; i.e. inchoate offences linked to this offence

legislation.description: The legislation associated to this offence (from actsAndSections in the SDRS response)

maxPeriodIsLife.description: Set to true if max period is life

maxPeriodOfIndictmentYears.description: Set to the max period of indictment in years

maxPeriodOfIndictmentMonths.description: Set to the max period of indictment in months

maxPeriodOfIndictmentWeeks.description: Set to the max period of indictment in weeks

maxPeriodOfIndictmentDays.description: Set to the max period of indictment in days

custodialIndicator.description: Yes if the offence caries a custodial sentence, Either if it does when tried as an indictment and No otherwise.

inListA.description: Schedule 15 Part 1 + Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life (exclude all offences that start on or after 28 June 2022)

inListB.description: SDS between 4 and 7 years : Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life + serious violent offences

inListC.description: Sec250 >7 years = List C: Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life + serious violent offences (same as List B)

inListD.description: Schedule 15 Part 1 + Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life

SdsOffenceDetails.description: Shows which (if any) PCSC Marker the offence relates to as well as any SDS early release exclusions for the offence

OffenceSdsExclusion.description: Categorises the offence based on the exclusion list it appears in

SdsExclusionLists.description: Contains the list of all the offences that are sexual, domestic abuse, national security, terrorism or violent

sexual.description: Offence falls under the Sexual category

sexualTrancheThree.description: Tranche Three Offence falls under the Sexual category

domesticAbuse.description: Offence falls under the Domestic Abuse category

domesticAbuseTrancheThree.description: Tranche Three Offence falls under the Domestic Abuse category

nationalSecurity.description: Offence falls under the National Security category

violent.description: Offence falls under the Violent category

terrorism.description: Offence falls under the Terrorism category

murderTrancheThree.description: Tranche Three Offence falls under the Murder category

listA.description: Schedule 15 Part 1 + Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life (exclude all offences that start on or after 28 June 2022)

listB.description: SDS between 4 and 7 years : Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life + serious violent offences

listC.description: Sec250 >7 years = List C: Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life + serious violent offences (same as List B)

listD.description: Schedule 15 Part 1 + Schedule 15 Part 2 that attract life

OffencePcscMarkers.description: Shows which (if any) PCSC Marker the offence relates to

MostRecentLoadResult.description: Details of the load by SDRS Cache

sdrsCache.description: Associated SDRS Cache - indicates the part of the SDRS load this status relates to

loadDate.description: The date and time of the load

lastSuccessfulLoadDate.description: The date and time of the most recent successful load; if the load was successful this is the same as the loadDate

NomisChangeHistory.description: This shows a change to NOMIS

code.description: This is set depending on the nomisChangeType - could be the code of the offence, statute or Home Office Stats

sentToNomisDate.description: The date this change was made in NOMIS
